v10_engine: signal at bar[i] close executed at bar[i+1] open
standalone_engine: signal at bar[i-1] close executed at bar[i] open
impact_notes: semantically identical
v10 applies spread+slip multiplicatively on price; standalone fills at raw open, cost applied separately
same as buy but reversed; v10 gets worse fill price, standalone fills at raw open
v10 separates spread/slip (in fill price) from fee (on fill_px notional); standalone lumps all into single rate on raw notional
different parameterization but same total cost can be achieved
standalone uses normalized equity; affects absolute trade sizes but not returns
semantically identical
semantically identical

v10_engine: at bar CLOSE within reporting window
impact_notes: v10 separates warmup from reporting; standalone records all bars

v10_engine: _EXPO_THRESHOLD = 0.005 (hardcoded in engine, applied to ALL signals)
impact_notes: v10 engine silently ignores exposure deltas < 0.5%; standalone only uses strategy-level threshold
both strategies use 0.05 (5%) but applied at different layers

v10_engine: engine: target < 0.005 AND btc_qty > 1e-8 → sell all
impact_notes: both ensure exit from flat is always allowed
both limit buy to available cash after cost
mathematically identical
identical
identical

v10_engine: real calendar time from close_time delta
impact_notes: v10 uses actual time; standalone assumes uniform bar spacing. Small difference for H4 data with gaps
display difference; the scoring function uses v10's percent format

v10_engine: configurable: no_trade (default) or allow_trade; based on report_start_ms
standalone_engine: skip bars before first all-finite indicator index
impact_notes: both exclude warmup from metrics but mechanisms differ

v10_engine: strict D1 alignment with no-lookahead (d1.close_time < h4.close_time)
impact_notes: v10 engine supports multi-timeframe; standalone is single-timeframe. VTREND E0 uses D1 data (for EMA regime in some configs); LATCH standalone does not

v10_engine: Trade records with entry/exit price, PnL, days_held
standalone_engine: TradeRecord with bar_index, timestamp, side, weight before/after
impact_notes: different trade semantics but both track costs
